title: Unable to use Podman from Pycharm due to API version string
body:
**Description**

Tried using the Podman REST API to enable using it as an execution backend for Pycharm.

1. Run podman API on a TCP port with `podman system service --time=0 tcp:localhost:2375 --log-level debug`
2. Configure Pycharm to connect to Docker by TCP with Engine API URL: `http://localhost:2375`

**Describe the results you received:**

This error is shown in Pycharm

```
Cannot connect: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: 1.0.0 can not be parsed
```
With the log level set to `debug`, I can see that it requested the `version` endpoint, which reports:

```
"APIVersion": "1.0.0"
```

It appears that this is a parsing error due to Docker API versions normally being formatted as `1.40`.

Not sure if Pycharm would accept `1.00`, but at least the parsing of the version would work, I guess.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Podman to be sufficiently compatible with the Docker API so that I can use it as a build backend for Pycharm.

One idea to allow hacking around clients with restrictions on API version (there may be many more of these besides Pycharm) might be to allow using a different TCP base URL to get it to report whatever version your clients needs. So if I e.g. put in `http://localhost:2375/v1.40` as your API base URL, that's the API version that would be reported by Podman.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.4
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 07:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Pycharm version: `2020.2`

comments.body:
Also reported this to Jetbrains here: https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-226650#focus=Comments-27-4345415.0-0 , since this could potentially be fixed there as well.

However, I suspect this can cause issues with other clients, so might be good to address it in Podman regardless.

Likely issue is that one of our unversioned endpoints is reporting Libpod
API version instead of the Docker compatible API version.

On Sat, Aug 15, 2020, 10:04 Tom Sweeney <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> @jwhonce <https://github.com/jwhonce> thoughts?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7327#issuecomment-674402528>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCFQIEGQGWTNGAPM2ATSA2IYTANCNFSM4QAHNLXQ>
> .
>

> Likely issue is that one of our unversioned endpoints is reporting Libpod API version instead of the Docker compatible API version.

It appears to affect versioned endpoints also. If I put in `http://localhost:2375/v1.40` I can see from the debug output that Pycharm requests `http://localhost:2375/v1.40/version`, which still says `"APIVersion":"1.0.0"`.
@jwhonce can you have a look at it? I believe you already have some battle scars in that big version matrix.
From the look of things, we're going to need a separate version endpoint for Compat that reports the Docker API version, not the Libpod one.

Problem with Jetbrain's Docker plugin is that version string is not parsed as semver string (what Podman returns). Docker API also does not strictly define that ApiVersion must be semver string.

I have patched Podman's `/version` endpoint (which BTW is already in compat package) to return Docker API version just to test if everything else works. Result is:
- Jetbrain's Docker plugin can now connect, and list all images
- Running container instances (started via CLI) are listed, but you cannot interact with them from UI
- New container instances cannot be started successfully from UI

I do not know what the problem is, but Podman debug output shows that only two endpoints are used:
`/containers/json?all=true`
`/images/json`

It is maybe out of scope, but Eclipse Docker plugin also does not work. Version endpoint is here not the problem, but /images/json. Podman returns here a json which does not comply with the Docker API specification. Docker API defines for example ParentId, SharedSize, VirtualSize, Labels and Containers attributes as mandatory, but Podman does not provide them. RepoDigests is called Digests in Podman's result. Because of that, json deserialisation in Eclipse Docker plugin fails.

Fixing `/version` endpoint is for sure a good thing, but still has no huge value because both popular IDEs will still not work.
If possible, fix also `/images/json` compat endpoint, that will help Eclipse plugin. Jetbrain's Docker plugin is not open-source, so you cannot do anything for it as long as it is not clear what the problem is.

@mlegenovic Can we move the discussion around omitted fields to another issue? To save on bandwidth when encoding JSON we have routinely omitted zero-value fields.  If this is causing decoding issues, we can revisit this design decision.
